response.justification: While Sarah demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 19+ years of development experience, there is a fundamental domain mismatch. The role requires 8–12 years of C++ expertise and industrial automation/cybersecurity knowledge; her resume shows only ~6 months of C++ at National Instruments and no industrial domain exposure. Her primary experience is full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript development, with current work focused on AI infrastructure—moving further from the core C++/systems requirements. The absence of IEC 62443, SDL, or secure development lifecycle expertise is a critical gap for this security-critical industrial role.
strengths: Extensive overall software development experience (19+ years) with proven ability to architect complex systems, including event-driven platforms
Strong track record of mentorship, knowledge-sharing, and agile practices (TDD, pairing, CI/CD); demonstrated commitment to code quality and team development
Versatile technical foundation across multiple domains (web, mobile, embedded, GIS) showing adaptability and broad problem-solving capability
concerns: Only ~6 months of explicit C++ experience at National Instruments; primary expertise is JavaScript/TypeScript and D lang, not the core C++ required for 8–12-year role specification
No demonstrated experience in industrial automation, IoT manufacturing, or automation machinery domains; no mention of IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial cybersecurity standards

Current role at re:cinq (full-stack TypeScript/Node.js, AI infrastructure) represents continued trajectory away from systems programming and C++, suggesting lower motivation for this C++-focused position

key_factors.factor: C++ depth (6 months vs. 8–12 years required)
Industrial automation & cybersecurity domain expertise (none documented)
Software engineering fundamentals, mentorship, and agile practices
